policies.title: Centralized Authentication Logging
policies.policy_text: Every successful and failed login attempt must be recorded with timestamp, source IP address, username, and authentication outcome.

policies.title: Password Storage Standard
policies.policy_text: Application passwords must be stored only as salted cryptographic hashes and must never be stored or transmitted in plaintext.

policies.title: Vendor Account Hardening
policies.policy_text: All default usernames and vendor-supplied passwords must be changed before any server or application is moved into production.

policies.title: Legacy Authentication Exception
policies.policy_text: Legacy authentication protocols may remain enabled if they are required by older business applications.

policies.title: Authentication Security Review
policies.policy_text: Authentication systems shall undergo a documented security review every year to verify their continued effectiveness.

policies.title: Login Rate Protection
policies.policy_text: Internet-facing login services must implement rate limiting or account lockout mechanisms to reduce brute-force attacks.

policies.title: Encrypted Credential Storage
policies.policy_text: User credentials must always be encrypted before being stored in the authentication database.

policies.title: Shared Operations Account
policies.policy_text: Operations engineers may use a common administrator account during emergency maintenance to simplify incident response.

policies.title: Session Timeout Policy
policies.policy_text: Authenticated user sessions must automatically expire after a defined period of inactivity.

policies.title: Authentication Event Monitoring
policies.policy_text: Authentication events shall be forwarded to the centralized SIEM platform for continuous monitoring and security alerting.

policies.title: Role-Based Access Assignment
policies.policy_text: User permissions shall be assigned strictly according to documented job roles and responsibilities.

policies.title: Default Deny for New Applications
policies.policy_text: All newly deployed applications must deny access by default until explicit permissions are granted.

policies.title: Privilege Elevation Approval
policies.policy_text: Temporary administrator privileges require documented approval and must expire automatically after the approved duration.

policies.title: API Authorization Enforcement
policies.policy_text: Authorization checks must be enforced by backend services and not rely solely on client-side validation.

policies.title: Quarterly Access Certification
policies.policy_text: Managers must review and certify employee access rights every quarter for systems containing sensitive information.

policies.title: Access Requests by Manager Approval
policies.policy_text: Employees may receive access after approval from their reporting manager.

policies.title: Permanent Administrator Access
policies.policy_text: System administrators shall retain permanent privileged access to all production systems for operational efficiency.

policies.title: Client-Side Permission Validation
policies.policy_text: User permissions may be validated only within the browser application to reduce backend processing overhead.

policies.title: Entitlement Review Process
policies.policy_text: Access entitlements shall be reviewed periodically to ensure they remain appropriate for each employee's responsibilities.

policies.title: Financial Duty Separation
policies.policy_text: The same employee must not both approve and execute high-value financial transactions.

policies.title: Automated Employee Onboarding
policies.policy_text: New user accounts shall be created only after receiving verified approval from the HR system or the employee's sponsoring manager.

policies.title: Employee Offboarding
policies.policy_text: All user accounts, VPN access, and application permissions must be disabled within 24 hours after an employee leaves the organization.

policies.title: Role Change Access Synchronization
policies.policy_text: Whenever an employee changes departments or job roles, their system permissions must be automatically updated to reflect their new responsibilities.

policies.title: Service Account Ownership
policies.policy_text: Every service account must have a designated employee responsible for its ownership, maintenance, and periodic review.

policies.title: Dormant Account Cleanup
policies.policy_text: Inactive user accounts that remain unused beyond the approved inactivity period shall be automatically disabled until reviewed.

policies.title: HR Identity Synchronization
policies.policy_text: Employee identity information should be synchronized with HR records whenever organizational updates occur.

policies.title: Temporary Contractor Accounts
policies.policy_text: Contractor accounts may remain active until the vendor notifies the company that the engagement has ended.

policies.title: Unnamed Service Accounts
policies.policy_text: Shared service accounts do not require an assigned owner because they are maintained by the infrastructure team.

policies.title: Identity Lifecycle Audit Trail
policies.policy_text: All account provisioning, modification, and deprovisioning activities shall be recorded for audit purposes.

policies.title: Authoritative Identity Source
policies.policy_text: Employee identity attributes shall be obtained from the organization's approved HR system before user accounts are created.

policies.title: Emergency Authentication Exception
policies.policy_text: During operational emergencies, authentication controls may be temporarily relaxed if business continuity would otherwise be affected.

policies.title: Operational Password Sharing
policies.policy_text: Critical infrastructure credentials may be shared among authorized team members when necessary to ensure uninterrupted service delivery.

policies.title: Flexible Session Duration
policies.policy_text: Applications may determine appropriate session timeout values based on user experience requirements.

policies.title: Alternative Authentication Methods
policies.policy_text: Legacy authentication methods may remain available for compatibility purposes when requested by business stakeholders.

policies.title: Manager Approved Privileged Access
policies.policy_text: Managers may grant privileged access whenever they determine it is appropriate for business operations.

policies.title: Discretionary Logging Policy
policies.policy_text: Systems should record security events whenever practical, taking storage limitations into consideration.

policies.title: Contractor Identity Lifecycle
policies.policy_text: Contractor accounts should be reviewed periodically and disabled when no longer required.

policies.title: Risk-Based MFA Exception
policies.policy_text: Multi-factor authentication may be omitted for trusted users accessing low-risk business systems.

policies.title: Administrative Access Convenience
policies.policy_text: Administrative accounts may remain active for future operational needs to reduce provisioning delays.

policies.title: Security Control Exception Process
policies.policy_text: Business units may implement compensating security practices that they believe provide equivalent protection without requiring formal approval.
